title: uwritestringsyssetting
text: uwritestringsyssetting Copies a string to a specified section of a specified ini file. Explain this code int uwritestringsyssetting (char*section, char*key, char*value, char*iniFile); Copy Parameters section Name of the section to which the string will be copied. If the section does not exist, it will be created. key Name of the key to be associated with the string. If the key does not exist in section , it will be created. If key is NULL, the entire section is deleted, including all keys within the section. value String to be written to the file. If NULL, the key pointed to by the key parameter is deleted. iniFile Name of the initialization file Return Values Table 1. Values returned by uwritestringsyssetting Value Meaning <>0 Success. The string was copied to the initialization file. 0 The function failed. To get extended error information, call the Windows function GetLastError . Use Microsoft Windows platforms only. Library: ulib Description This function is a wrapper for the Windows API function WritePrivateProfileString and follows the same rules. For extensive information about this function, visit the Microsoft DN technical documentation site . Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> uwritestringsyssetting("mysection","mykey,"myvalue",ugetprofile()); Copy Related Links ugetprofile Return a pointer to the name of the current .ini file. ureadstringsyssetting Read a string from the specified section of a specified ini file. Calling Windows GetLastError() Function In a 3GL signature used to call Windows API functions, you can define a pseudo-operation with the name ULASTERROR rather than calling GetLastError().
